title: Series B Term Sheet
description: An outline specifying terms for a Series B preferred stock financing round, covering valuation, liquidation preferences, and board seats.
whatsCovered: Series B investment size and pre-money valuation
Dividend preferences and standard liquidation rights
Voting agreements, protective provisions, and board seats
Exclusivity period and closing transaction costs
Governing law and non-binding status of the document
whenToUse: Use this term sheet outline when negotiating a Series B equity financing round with venture capital firms or lead institutional investors.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

fields.label: Investor Group / Lead VC
fields.placeholder: e.g. Apex Venture Partners Fund II

clauses.heading: SERIES B TERM SHEET
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for Series B Term Sheet. The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. Investment and Valuation Covenants
clauses.body: Investor shall purchase Series B Preferred Stock for an aggregate amount of {investment_amount}. The pre-money valuation of the Company is established at {pre_money_valuation}. Board seat representations shall invite {board_seats} as director nominee.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Is this term sheet legally binding?
faq.answer: No, except for clauses regarding confidentiality, expenses, and exclusivity, term sheets are non-binding outlines subject to final diligence.

faq.question: What is a 1x liquidation preference?
faq.answer: It ensures Series B investors get their full investment back first before common shareholders receive proceeds during a liquidation event.
